rows.definition: ContextTokensLeft is the session.Budget field holding the remaining prompt/context-token budget a served session has before context-budget exhaustion triggers a reset directive.
rows.distinction: Not the model's fixed context-window limit (context-window); it is the live remaining count that the scheduler debits down each turn.

rows.definition: ContextTokens is the per-turn usage field on gateway.SessionUsage / session.Usage carrying the prompt/context-window token count to debit from the session budget.
rows.distinction: Not the remaining budget (ContextTokensLeft (session budget)); it is the consumed amount reported for one turn that is subtracted from that remainder.

rows.definition: RequestContextChange is the recall.Session method that applies a safe, negative-only context mutation — recording a tombstone that suppresses one page from future model-visible recall without deleting bytes.
rows.distinction: Not the request envelope itself (contextchangerequest) nor the gateway wire route; it is the recall-kernel apply operation that consumes that request.

rows.definition: ContextActionTombstone is the recall.ContextAction constant ("tombstone_page") naming the one shipped, deliberately negative-only context mutation that narrows what may enter future context.
rows.distinction: Not the ContextChangeRequest envelope that carries it (contextchangerequest); it is the action-type enum value inside that envelope.

rows.definition: ctxplanbench is the cmd/ctxplanbench benchmark that measures the ctxplan planned VIEW (compaction count, recovered tokens) over real Claude Code session transcripts.
rows.distinction: Not the ctxplan planner engine itself (ctxplan); it is the measurement harness that replays real transcripts through that engine.

rows.definition: ContextChanges is the recall.Session accessor (and Manifest field) returning the applied, durable context-control ledger — the list of tombstone rows recorded against the core image.
rows.distinction: Not a pending ContextChangeRequest (contextchangerequest); it is the append-only ledger of changes that were already applied.

rows.definition: ContextPollutionRate is the bench KPI computed as the rate of ctxmmu quarantines per tool call in the gates-on run.
rows.distinction: Not the context-mmu quarantine mechanism itself (context-mmu); it is a measured ratio reported over that mechanism's events.

rows.definition: ctxdemo is the cmd/ctxdemo live on-box demonstrator that runs a catalog of multi-agent shapes to show fak's prefill/KV-reuse value, reporting per-scenario max context and fak-vs-tuned token work.
rows.distinction: Not the context PLANNER demonstrator (ctxplandemo); ctxdemo exercises the multi-agent KV-reuse axis, not the planned-view lowering.

rows.definition: CtxViewBudget is the gateway.Config field that, when > 0, wires the ctxplan context planner into the live serve/guard loop and materializes each turn's history as an O(1) planned view under this resident-token budget.
rows.distinction: Not the planner that consumes it (ctxviewplanner) nor the view it emits (ctxview); it is the single resident-token budget knob that turns that planner on.

rows.definition: ProveRecallInput is the vcachechain struct holding the caller-supplied calibration (prefix tokens, unit tokens, read-multiplier, siblings) that the recall economics proof is run over.
rows.distinction: Not the proof's output (prove-recall / recallproof); it is the input parameter bundle fed into that proof.

rows.definition: cmdRecall is the cmd/fak handler for `fak recall` — the offline session-recall demo that writes a session through the gate, reloads it in a fresh Session, and demonstrates the rung-0/rung-4 poison-refusal guarantees.
rows.distinction: Not the recall subsystem/concept (recall); it is the one CLI verb that drives a demo over it.

rows.definition: CompactionFired is the gateway metrics counter of compaction attempts that fired — the turn body was rewritten with the protected prefix shipped byte-identical.
rows.distinction: Not the compaction mechanism itself (compaction) and not the cache_read recorder (recordCompactionCacheRead); it counts only successful fires.

rows.definition: contextBudgetAxis is the cmd/fak formatter that renders a context-token budget value as a short axis cell ("inf" for <0, "off" for 0, else the number) for ps/overview/session rows.
rows.distinction: Not the underlying budget field (ContextTokensLeft (session budget)); it is the display-string formatter over that field.

rows.definition: contextBudgetTokens is the `fak guard --context-budget-tokens` flag that seeds the guard session with a prompt/context-token budget so exhaustion returns a reset directive with a continuation id.
rows.distinction: Not the runtime remaining field it seeds (ContextTokensLeft (session budget)); it is the operator-set CLI knob supplying the initial value.

rows.definition: ContextTokensCap is the session.Budget field recording the configured context-budget size — the denominator the pre-exhaustion warning measures consumed-share against (0 means no context budget).
rows.distinction: Not the remaining count (ContextTokensLeft (session budget)); it is the fixed cap the remaining is measured as a fraction of.

rows.definition: ContextWindowTokens is the agent.Usage method that computes the prompt/context size to count against a long-session budget, adding Anthropic cache_read/cache_creation tokens back onto input_tokens.
rows.distinction: Not the context-window concept or any fixed limit (context-window); it is the per-turn accounting function that derives the chargeable context size.

rows.definition: ctxbench is the cmd/ctxbench tool that runs fak's security gates over a corpus of tool calls and reports the injection/quarantine outcomes.
rows.distinction: Not a context-planning benchmark (ctxplanbench (planner bench cmd)); ctxbench exercises the safety gates over a tool-call corpus, not the planned view.

rows.definition: handleFakContextChange is the gateway HTTP handler for POST /v1/fak/context/change that records a requester-initiated tombstone against a persisted recall core image.
rows.distinction: Not the recall-kernel apply method (RequestContextChange (recall)); it is the wire route that decodes the request and delegates to that method.

rows.definition: freshContextTokens is the cmd/fak guard budget-restarter field holding the fresh context-token budget to reseed onto a continuation trace after a budget-triggered managed reset.
rows.distinction: Not the live remaining budget (ContextTokensLeft (session budget)); it is the fixed reseed value applied when a session is restarted.

rows.definition: InjectionInContext is the agent loop-result boolean recording that a tool result carrying a prompt injection actually reached the model in the context.
rows.distinction: Not the ctxmmu quarantine ledger that prevents it (context-mmu); it is a per-run outcome flag in the agent A/B harness.

rows.definition: longctxbench is the cmd/longctxbench tool that renders the exact, contention-free token-work floor for ultra-long-context / agent-city regimes across a prefix/turns/agents ladder.
rows.distinction: Not the planned-view bench (ctxplanbench (planner bench cmd)); longctxbench computes a deterministic work floor, not a measured plan over transcripts.

rows.definition: MaxContext is the swebench.Geometry method giving the largest context length a workload geometry reaches, computed as Prefix + Turns*(Decode+Result).
rows.distinction: Not the model's fixed context-window limit (context-window); it is a measured per-workload peak context length.

rows.definition: NewRecallBackend is the memq constructor that builds the negative-only recall backend — a tombstone store that drives suppression through recall.RequestContextChange persisted to a directory.
rows.distinction: Not the recall subsystem broadly (recall); it is the memq-layer adapter that exposes a backend over it.

rows.definition: PlanRecall is the vcachechain M4 entry point that, given a prefix DAG, a recall request, and an enable flag, returns the recall verdict plus the economics and cold-tail replay schedule.
rows.distinction: Not the economic proof of net-positivity (prove-recall / recallproof); PlanRecall decides and schedules a recall, whereas the proof only certifies the math.

rows.definition: RopeOrigContext is the model.Config field (rope_scaling_original_max_position_embeddings) holding the original pre-scaling max position / context length used as the reference when applying RoPE scaling.
rows.distinction: Not a runtime context budget or the served context-window limit (context-window); it is a RoPE-scaling reference length read from the model config.

rows.definition: ctxresidency is the internal package providing the context-residency query: a pure, witnessable READ that composes kvmmu, ctxmmu, and cachemeta to classify each span resident/evictable/held with its evict blast radius.
rows.distinction: Not the write-side kernels it reads (context-mmu, kv-mmu); it is the off-write-path read projection that reconciles with their counters.

rows.definition: ctxplandemo is the cmd/ctxplandemo runnable demonstrator for the context PLANNER, treating a transcript as a plannable store and showing the planned view it materializes.
rows.distinction: Not the multi-agent KV-reuse demo (ctxdemo (multi-agent demo cmd)); ctxplandemo demonstrates the ctxplan planner's view, not cross-agent prefill reuse.
